name: Symptoms from work chemicals showed up months later, is it too late in Ogden?
description: Deadlines start at diagnosis for delayed occupational injuries.

mainEntity.name: Symptoms from work chemicals showed up months later, is it too late in Ogden?

acceptedAnswer.text: Deadlines start at diagnosis for delayed occupational injuries.
acceptedAnswer.description: **No, for occupational diseases like delayed chemical effects, the deadline starts from diagnosis or awareness.** Utah allows up to 8 years for occupational claims if linked to work.[1][2] Key factors: medical proof of causation, exposure history, and prompt filing post-symptoms. Get specialist eval for latency; preserve old employment records. Attorney extends effective timelines via disputes.
